title: Inspector
description: An MCP server that lets a coding agent see, operate, and test the app it built across web, Electron, Android, and iOS, and feed back reproducible, source-linked findings.

pages.title: Overview & Quickstart
pages.description: What Inspector is, the gap it fills, and how to install and run it.

pages.title: Architecture & Trust Boundaries
pages.description: The SurfaceAdapter abstraction, execution planes, and the security model.

pages.title: MCP Contract
pages.description: The tool surface (core vs advanced profile), annotations, and the session state machine.

pages.title: Core Loop & Session Lifecycle
pages.description: perceive to ground to act to check, grounding-by-id, and session launch/teardown.

pages.title: Detection & Evidence Channels
pages.description: Deterministic detection, the DOM audit, finding synthesis, and reproducibility.

pages.title: Surface Adapters
pages.description: Web, Electron, Android, iOS, and native macOS adapters and their planes.

pages.title: Cartographer Method
pages.description: Region-decomposed deterministic lens sweep and multi-agent bug finding.

pages.title: Evaluation Protocol
pages.description: How Inspector is scored: one-to-one matching, the system under test, and fixtures.

pages.title: Devin Integration
pages.description: Hand a finding to Devin to open a fix PR, and poll its status.

pages.title: Contributing & Changelog
pages.description: Development setup, conventions, and the change history.
